situation: audit-2026-07-01-003 H-001 finding: termux-listener.py accepts TCP messages and passes them unsanitized to termux-notification. Priority field not validated against whitelist; title/message not stripped of control chars (newline injection risk for termux-notification flag parsing); no length cap causes silent UTF-8 truncation on >1024B recv.
action: Applied route-fix per audit remediation plan: (1) added VALID_PRIORITIES whitelist {default,high,low,min,max} with fallback to 'default' on mismatch; (2) added _sanitize() helper stripping \x00-\x1f/\x7f + collapsing whitespace + explicit truncation at MAX_MESSAGE_LEN=500 with ellipsis marker; (3) title capped at 100 chars; (4) send_notification now checks subprocess returncode and logs non-zero exits + TimeoutExpired separately; (5) empty-after-sanitize message skipped. Single file change, no public interface contract altered.
outcome_evidence: python3 -c 'import ast; ast.parse(open("termux-listener.py").read())' passed syntax check; opt-worktree.sh committed to new worktree opt-runtime-listeners-1782902553 (route-fix auto-detected — runtime-listeners area not in .claude/public-interfaces.txt); branch pushed to origin/auto/opt-runtime-listeners-1782902553; commit verified via 'git log --oneline -1' in worktree shows fix message.

派生 fix #3+#4 bundle: M-001 log truncation hardening + M-002 AGE init (route-fix, same file watchdog.sh)

audit_findings.finding: TCP message fields (priority/title/message) passed unsanitized to subprocess; priority not whitelisted; control chars enable termux-notification flag injection; recv(1024) silently truncates mid-UTF8
audit_findings.remediation: Priority whitelist + re.sub control-char strip + explicit length cap + subprocess returncode check
